name: file-read
content:
# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

tool_calls.ArgumentsJson:
{"path": "C:\Users\aralm\repo\custom-agents\tools\weather.py", "content": """"
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"}

{"path": "C:\Users\aralm\repo\custom-agents\tools\weather.py", "content": """"
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"}

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

{"path": "C:\Users\aralm\repo\custom-agents\tools\weather.py", "content": """"
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"}

Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

{"path": "C:\Users\aralm\repo\custom-agents\tools\weather.py", "content": """"
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"}

ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""

"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:

        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""

        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""

"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""

    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

    """

import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""

import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """

    Args:

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python tools/weather.py <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python tools/weather.py New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    # Write UTF-8 bytes directly to stdout buffer
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(result.encode('utf-8'))
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(b'\n')


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python tools/weather.py <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python tools/weather.py New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    # Write UTF-8 bytes directly to stdout buffer
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(result.encode('utf-8'))
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(b'\n')


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python tools/weather.py <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python tools/weather.py New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    # Write UTF-8 bytes directly to stdout buffer
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(result.encode('utf-8'))
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(b'\n')


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python tools/weather.py <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python tools/weather.py New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    # Write UTF-8 bytes directly to stdout buffer
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(result.encode('utf-8'))
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(b'\n')


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# default do commands; 
prompt() -> prompts the user and returns it's content
turn() -> runs an agent turn that will keep looping until there's no more tool calls.

# model providers
ollama -> local ollama no auth
openrouter -> reading $env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY
# default tag types:
model: list of fallbacks -> fallback models, if first fails try next in the format provider@modelname.
label:name -> marks a location
role:role -> marks the begining of a new message in the chat message list for that role.
do:command -> runs a do command and injects the output in the current history.
tools:tool1,tool2 -> list of tools that are made available to the assistant from that point onward
choose:label1|label2 -> the text following that label until the next label (except for [$history]) or eof will be sent to complete without the history. Then check if the completion returns one and only one of the outputs and go to that label, do so by checking if output includes each option in case insensitive and removing spaces for compatibility, otherwise try again up to 3 times or respond with error.
shell: runs a shell command from the working path, eg; [shell:```si-search memory-api.json search -q "$prompt" -k 10 -min_score 0.75```] the command is inside the ``` markers.
goto: goto the label
partial:critique_something.md -> appends that md at the label position.

# default tools
file-read -> reads the content of a file and injects it into the assistant, should have a max_length value
file-write -> writes content to a file, should create the folder if not existing recursivelly, if relative use working path and allow for ~ for user root.
file-search -> searches for files from a folder, with filter and allow recurse. 
shell -> runs a shell command and returns the output.

# default variables
there's some variables to be replaced when processing the agent.md when found between {$var}

$workingPath -> the starting path for this agent.
$datetime -> current date and time
$prompt -> the last user prompt acquired using do:prompt()
$completion -> the last assistant text completion

# other stuff
empty lines in the agent.md are ignored and not included.

This is kind of a prompt and control flow application, we keep moving through the template and prompting the user, performing turns, changing the context eg; tools etc and keeping a message history we keep appending to. 
Some messages may be built from multiple variables, calls to eg; do:prompt so we will keep appending the content. 
In case of turn if the current role is not assistant we will append it as a new message though. 

# project type and structure
this should be some kind of orchestrator you bind to for prompts to the user and outputs so we can wire this into multiple  project types, eg; api or cli. For now just build a CLI for it, but everything else should be a module and a class. 
sources inside /src
The cli should have a --prompt <initial user prompt for the first do:prompt()>
Should build as an exe file and be a .net project. Add a publish.ps1 that publishes the cli to /publish and adds it to the user path.
If there's no prompt at the end and not looping we just end, do not keep asking the user.
The completions should be streamed.

# logging
it should generate a json lines log per agent conversation with each full request, tool call and tool response.

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python -m tools.weather <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python -m tools.weather New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    print(result)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""
Weather tool for fetching current weather from wttr.in
"""
import requests
import sys


def get_weather(location: str, format_type: str = "2") -> str:
    """
    Get current weather for a location using wttr.in API.
    
    Args:
        location: The location to get weather for (e.g., "New York", "London", "Paris")
        format_type: The format for the response (default "2" for compact)
    
    Returns:
        Weather information as a string
    """
    url = f"http://wttr.in/{location}?format={format_type}"
    try:
        r = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        return r.text.strip()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        return f"Error fetching weather: {e}"


def main():
    """CLI entry point for weather tool."""
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: python tools/weather.py <location> [format]")
        print("Example: python tools/weather.py New_York")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    location = sys.argv[1]
    format_type = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else "2"
    
    result = get_weather(location, format_type)
    # Write UTF-8 bytes directly to stdout buffer
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(result.encode('utf-8'))
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(b'\n')


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
